an1.31:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing that, when untamed, leads to such great harm as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.31:1.2: An untamed mind [*citta*], bhikkhus, leads to great harm.”
an1.32:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing that, when tamed, leads to such great benefit as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.32:1.2: A tamed mind [*citta*], bhikkhus, leads to great benefit.”
an1.33:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing that, when unguarded, leads to such great harm as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.33:1.2: An unguarded mind [*citta*], bhikkhus, leads to great harm.”
an1.34:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing that, when guarded, leads to such great benefit as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.34:1.2: A guarded mind [*citta*], bhikkhus, leads to great benefit.”
an1.35:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing that, when unprotected, leads to such great harm as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.35:1.2: An unprotected mind [*citta*], bhikkhus, leads to great harm.”
an1.36:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing that, when protected, leads to such great benefit as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.36:1.2: A protected mind [*citta*], bhikkhus, leads to great benefit.”
an1.37:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing that, when unrestrained, leads to such great harm as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.37:1.2: An unrestrained mind [*citta*], bhikkhus, leads to great harm.”
an1.38:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing that, when restrained, leads to such great benefit as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.38:1.2: A restrained mind [*citta*], bhikkhus, leads to great benefit.”
an1.39:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing that, when untamed, unguarded, unprotected, and unrestrained, leads to such great harm as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.39:1.2: An untamed, unguarded, unprotected, and unrestrained mind [*citta*], bhikkhus, leads to great harm.”
an1.40:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing that, when tamed, guarded, protected, and restrained, leads to such great benefit as this: the mind [*citta*].
an1.40:1.2: A tamed, guarded, protected, and restrained mind [*citta*], bhikkhus, leads to great benefit.”
an1.40:1.4: The Untamed Chapter is the fourth.
